diffs.diff:
--- a/model.py
+++ b/model.py
@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@
         return self.classifier(features)

 

 class GeneralizedCrossEntropy(nn.Module):

-    def __init__(self, num_classes=100, q=0.9):

+    def __init__(self, num_classes=100, q=0.7):

         super(GeneralizedCrossEntropy, self).__init__()

         self.q = q

         self.num_classes = num_classes

@@ -39,18 +39,20 @@
         gce_loss = (1 - torch.pow(prob_true, self.q)) / self.q

         return gce_loss.mean()

 

-class MeanAbsoluteError(nn.Module):

-    def __init__(self, num_classes=100):

-        super(MeanAbsoluteError, self).__init__()

+class FocalLoss(nn.Module):

+    def __init__(self, alpha=1, gamma=2, num_classes=100):

+        super(FocalLoss, self).__init__()

+        self.alpha = alpha

+        self.gamma = gamma

         self.num_classes = num_classes

-        self.eps = 1e-7

 

     def forward(self, pred, targets):

         # Apply softmax to get probabilities

         prob = torch.softmax(pred, dim=1)

-        # Create one-hot encoding of targets

-        one_hot = torch.zeros_like(prob)

-        one_hot.scatter_(1, targets.unsqueeze(1), 1)

-        # Calculate MAE loss

-        mae_loss = 1.0 - torch.sum(one_hot * prob, dim=1)

-        return mae_loss.mean()
+        # Get the probability of the correct class

+        prob_true = prob[torch.arange(targets.size(0)), targets]

+        # Calculate focal loss

+        focal_weight = self.alpha * torch.pow(1 - prob_true, self.gamma)

+        ce_loss = -torch.log(prob_true + 1e-7)  # Add epsilon for numerical stability

+        focal_loss = focal_weight * ce_loss

+        return focal_loss.mean()
